preamble.summary: God calls Moses from the burning bush. On the way back, Mastema attacks Moses at the inn — Jubilees' answer to the mysterious Exodus 4:24-26. The plagues fall. Mastema is bound at the Exodus. The Red Sea crossing.
preamble.remarkable: This chapter resolves one of the Torah's most puzzling passages by identifying Mastema as the attacker at the inn, not God. Mastema's binding parallels the binding of Azazel in 1 Enoch.
preamble.friction: The substitution of Mastema for God is a significant theological reinterpretation.
preamble.connections: Exodus 3:1-4:31; Exodus 7-12 (plagues); 1 Enoch 10 (binding of Azazel); Revelation 20:2.

verses.text_reference: And thou didst dwell in Midian five weeks and one year.
verses.rendering: You lived in Midian for five weeks and one year. You returned to Egypt in the second week, in the second year of the fiftieth jubilee.

verses.text_reference: And thou knowest what prince Mastema desired to do to thee at the inn.
verses.rendering: You know what the prince Mastema tried to do to you when you were returning to Egypt — when he met you at the inn.
translator_notes: This is Jubilees' interpretation of Exodus 4:24-26 — Mastema, not God, is the attacker.

key_terms.note: The chief demonic spirit, identified as the attacker at the inn — resolving the theological puzzle of Exodus 4:24-26

verses.text_reference: Did he not seek to slay thee?
verses.rendering: Did he not try with all his power to kill you and rescue the Egyptians from your hand?

verses.text_reference: And I delivered thee out of his hand.
verses.rendering: But I delivered you from his hand. You then performed the signs and wonders in Egypt.

verses.text_reference: And the Lord smote them with plagues.
verses.rendering: The Lord struck them with blood, frogs, lice, swarms of flies, diseased livestock, boils, hail, locusts, and darkness.

verses.text_reference: And He slew all the firstborn, and Mastema's works were destroyed.
verses.rendering: He killed all their firstborn and destroyed all their livestock. The Lord sent angels to destroy everything Mastema had created.
Prince Mastema was bound and imprisoned, kept away from the children of Israel so he could not accuse them.
translator_notes: Mastema's binding during the Exodus parallels the binding of Azazel in 1 Enoch 10:4-6.

verses.text_reference: And on that night the Lord commanded us to pass through Egypt.
verses.rendering: That night the Lord commanded us to lead all the angels through Egypt to strike down every firstborn.

verses.text_reference: And against all the gods of Egypt He executed judgments.
verses.rendering: Against all the gods of Egypt the Lord executed judgments and destroyed them.

verses.text_reference: And Mastema incited the Egyptians to pursue.
verses.rendering: Mastema incited the Egyptians to pursue Israel with all their chariots and horses.
translator_notes: Mastema retains enough influence to incite the pursuit despite being bound.

verses.text_reference: And I stood between the Egyptians and Israel.
verses.rendering: I stood between the Egyptians and Israel. We delivered Israel from Mastema's hand. The Lord brought them through the sea on dry land.
translator_notes: The angelic narrator reveals the cosmic battle dimension of the Exodus.

verses.text_reference: And all who pursued were cast into the sea.
verses.rendering: All who pursued Israel — the Lord cast them into the depths of the sea, as he had destroyed Sodom.

verses.text_reference: And He destroyed a million warriors in the sea.
verses.rendering: He took vengeance on a million soldiers and a million horsemen in the sea. On the fourteenth we bound Mastema.

verses.text_reference: And on the fifteenth we released him to pursue.
verses.rendering: On the fifteenth we released him toward the Egyptians to pursue Israel.
translator_notes: Mastema bound on Passover night (14th) and released on the 15th.

verses.text_reference: And the Lord saved Israel that day.
verses.rendering: On that day the Lord saved Israel from the hand of the Egyptians.

verses.text_reference: And Israel walked on dry ground through the sea.
verses.rendering: All the children of Israel walked on dry ground through the middle of the sea. The sea stood as a wall on their right and left.

verses.text_reference: And the Lord saved Israel from Egypt and Mastema.
verses.rendering: The Lord saved Israel that day — from Egypt, from Mastema, and from all the peoples of Egypt.

verses.text_reference: And Israel saw the Egyptians dead on the shore.
verses.rendering: Israel saw the Egyptians lying dead on the seashore. Israel witnessed the mighty hand of the Lord.

verses.text_reference: And the people feared the Lord and sang praises.
verses.rendering: The people feared the Lord and believed in him and in his servant Moses. They sang praises to the Lord.
